meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills: Escape Tutorial Hell & Build Real Apps
content.seo_description: Break free from basic tutorials. Master Claude Code and Antigravity to build professional-grade AI applications. A beginner-focused guide for student developers.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building isolated features, not systems. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you move from passive coding to AI-assisted architectural engineering. This setup automates boilerplate, enforces clean patterns, and accelerates your development velocity, allowing you to focus on logic, scalability, and high-impact project outcomes.
content.intro:
The screen glows in the dark, reflecting the same boilerplate code you’ve written a thousand times. You have completed the tutorials, but your portfolio feels hollow—a collection of 'to-do' lists that never leave your local environment. You are caught in the gravity of simple apps, unable to bridge the gap between 'student code' and 'production-ready software.'

It is time to change your workflow. By leveraging Claude Code as your pair programmer and Antigravity as your architectural engine, you stop typing and start orchestrating. This isn't just about writing code faster; it is about shifting your mindset from a student typing lines to an engineer architecting solutions that actually work.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Establish a clean, robust repository structure to ensure your AI agent understands the project's architectural context from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional project structure with best-practice folders for /src, /tests, and /config. Ensure a strict separation of concerns.

steps.title: Defining Core Logic
steps.description: Translate your high-level project requirements into executable business logic using AI-assisted scaffolding.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my requirement for a task management system and generate the core data models and service layer logic with type safety.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a service layer for task management using TypeScript and Zod schemas'

steps.title: Architectural Pattern Enforcement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to ensure your code follows modern design patterns like Dependency Injection or Repository patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current service implementation and refactor it to use the Repository pattern for better testability and modularity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor 'Apply Repository pattern to current service layer'

steps.title: Automated Test Generation
steps.description: Never write a test file manually again. Let the AI generate high-coverage test suites for your new features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive Jest test suite for the newly created service layer, including edge cases and error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Generate unit tests for task.service.ts covering all edge cases'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement & Deployment
steps.description: Finalize your feature by letting the AI debug and optimize your code for production performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a final code review for performance bottlenecks and security vulnerabilities in the current codebase.

pro_tips: Always provide a 'Context File' (.context) to Claude Code so it knows your existing project style guide before it starts writing.
Use Antigravity's 'diff mode' to manually approve every change; never let the AI push code without your eyes on the implementation.
Treat Claude Code as a junior developer: give it specific constraints, not just vague goals, to avoid 'hallucinated' dependencies.
Run 'claude explain' on your own code to see if the AI understands your logic; if it can't explain it, your code is too complex.
Leverage the 'checkpoint' feature in your CLI to revert instantly if an AI-driven refactor breaks your core build.
content.next_steps: After completing your first feature, deploy to Vercel or Netlify to see your app live. Then, choose one complex feature to add, such as user authentication or a database integration, to solidify your new architectural skills.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student following instructions; you are an engineer building the future. Stop waiting for permission to build something great. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
